definition: The cosmic and moral order governing individual and societal actions.
historical_meaning: In Sangam literature and Thirukkural, Aram is the first and most fundamental of the Three Aims of Life (Mupporu'l): Aram (virtue), Porul (wealth), Inbam (love). It corresponds broadly to Dharma and encompasses all righteous acts performed without expectation of reward. Thirukkural's first book (Aram) has 38 chapters codifying it.
related_concepts: Anbu, Arul, Kollamai, Ozhukkam, Vaimai, Igai
example_sentence: அறத்தாறின் இல்வாழ்க்கை ஆற்றின் புறத்தாறின் போஒய்ப் பெறுவது எவன்.
example_translation: If domestic life is lived on virtue's path, what more is gained by other paths?

english: Affection, love, the foundation of Aram
definition: The emotional bond of love that is the root of all virtue.
historical_meaning: Thirukkural Chapter 8 declares Anbu as the essential quality of humanity: 'Those who lack love claim all for themselves; those who have love give even their bones.' It is the animating force of domestic virtue and the foundation of Aram.
example_sentence: அன்பிலார் எல்லாம் தமக்குரியர் அன்புடையார் என்பும் உரியர் பிறர்க்கு.
example_translation: The loveless claim everything for themselves; the loving give even their bones to others.

definition: Benevolence and compassion extended beyond personal bonds to all living beings.
historical_meaning: Arul (Chapter 25 of Thirukkural) is the higher form of Anbu expanded universally. 'Arul-selva'm selvathu'l selva'm' — the wealth of grace is the highest wealth. It is a prerequisite for Ahimsa (Kollamai) and vegetarianism.
example_sentence: அருட்செல்வம் செல்வத்துள் செல்வம் பொருட்செல்வம் பூரியார் கண்ணும் உள.
example_translation: The wealth of grace is the highest; material wealth can be owned even by the basest men.

english: Fate, karmic destiny, the power of past deeds
definition: The inexorable law of cause and effect resulting from accumulated past actions.
historical_meaning: Thirukkural Chapter 38 (Oozh) states: 'What is stronger than fate? Even if one devises another plan, fate will forestall it.' Oozh represents karma — the deterministic force of past deeds that overrides conscious planning.
example_sentence: ஊழில் பெருவலி யாவுள மற்றொன்று சூழினும் தான்முந் துறும்.
example_translation: What power surpasses fate? Even with another plan, fate comes first.

definition: Rigorous self-restraint and voluntary endurance of pain to gain spiritual power.
historical_meaning: Thirukkural Chapter 27: 'Bearing one's own pain and not causing harm to others is the true nature of penance.' Thavam is both moral non-injury (Ahimsa) and physical endurance — the Sangam tradition valued its transformative power.
example_sentence: உற்றநோய் நோற்றல் உயிர்க்குறுகண் செய்யாமை அற்றே தவத்திற்கு உரு.
example_translation: Bearing one's own pain and not causing harm to others is the true form of penance.

definition: Aesthetic, sensual, and emotional pleasure, particularly in love (Akam).
historical_meaning: Third of the Three Aims (Mupporu'l). Inbam corresponds to Kamam (Sanskrit) and encompasses the complete joy of love and union. It is the entire subject of Thirukkural Book 3 (Kamattuppal) and all Akam poetry.
example_sentence: இன்பம் கடலினும் பெரிதே புணர்ச்சி பிரிவின் அவலத்தை மாற்றும்.
example_translation: The joy of union is larger than the ocean, curing the sorrow of separation.

definition: The lasting reputation earned through virtuous deeds and generosity.
historical_meaning: In Sangam heroic poetry, Pukazh (fame) outlasts physical death. The poet Kaniyan Pungundranar in Purananuru and Avvaiyar both emphasize that fame through generous deeds is the only true legacy of a hero.
example_sentence: ஈதல் இசைபட வாழ்தல் அதுவல்லது ஊதியம் இல்லை உயிர்க்கு.
example_translation: Giving to the needy and living with fame — that is the only true gain for a living soul.

definition: Economic prosperity acquired through righteous means to sustain life and duty.
historical_meaning: Second of the Mupporu'l. Thirukkural Book 2 (Porul) covers political science, statecraft, diplomacy, and economics. Porul is valid only when earned by Aram. 'Arut-selva'm selvathu'l selva'm, porut-selva'm puuriyaar kannum ula.'
example_sentence: அருட்செல்வம் செல்வத்துள் செல்வம் பொருட்செல்வம் பூரியார் கண்ணும் உள.
example_translation: The wealth of grace is the highest; material wealth exists even in the basest men.

definition: Adherence to the ethical code established by virtuous society.
historical_meaning: Thirukkural Chapter 14: 'Right conduct leads to excellence; therefore it must be guarded more zealously than life itself.' Ozhukkam is ranked above even learning (Kattru) because conduct without learning is superior to learning without conduct.
example_sentence: ஒழுக்கம் விழுப்பம் தரலான் ஒழுக்கம் உயிரினும் ஓம்பப் படும்.
example_translation: Right conduct brings excellence; it must be guarded more zealously than life.

definition: Speaking words that are entirely free from causing harm to any living being.
historical_meaning: Thirukkural Chapter 30: 'Truthfulness is speaking words that cause no harm to any. Even falsehood is truthfulness if it produces faultless good.' This uniquely defines truth by its consequences, not just its factual content.
example_sentence: வாய்மை எனப்படுவது யாதெனின் யாதொன்றும் தீமை இலாத சொலல்.
example_translation: Truthfulness means speaking words wholly free of harm to any creature.

definition: The supreme virtue of not destroying any life.
historical_meaning: Thirukkural Chapter 33: 'Not killing is the chief virtue; killing is the cause of all other vices.' Thirukkural Ch 26 adds that eating flesh is incompatible with Arul. This precedes Jain/Buddhist Ahimsa in Tamil intellectual tradition.
example_sentence: அறவினை யாதெனின் கொல்லாமை கோறல் பிறவினை எல்லாம் தரும்.
example_translation: Not killing is the chief virtue; killing causes all other vices.

definition: Giving to the poor without expectation — the only worthy use of wealth.
historical_meaning: Thirukkural Chapter 23: 'Giving to the poor is true charity; all other giving has an expectation of return.' Igai is the Akam counterpart of Kodai (Puram giving). It is unconditional giving as moral duty.
example_sentence: வறியார்க்கு ஒன்று ஈவதே ஈகை மற்றெல்லாம் குறியெதிர்ப்பை நீரது உடைத்து.
example_translation: Giving to the poor is true charity; all other giving is done with expectation of return.

definition: Giving wealth, food, and gifts — especially by kings to poets and warriors.
historical_meaning: Kodai is the Puram virtue of generosity, inseparable from valor (Viram). Sangam kings were evaluated by their Kodai: Chera Uthiyan Cheralathan, Chola Karikal Peruvalathan, and Pandya Nedunchezhiyan are praised for it.
example_sentence: ஈயாது வாழ்தல் புன்மை ஆகும் ஈதல் வலியார் செல்வம்.
example_translation: To live without giving is baseness; generosity is the wealth of the powerful.

definition: Preserving one's honor and dignity even at the cost of life.
historical_meaning: Thirukkural Chapter 97: 'He who cannot tolerate shame is the true noble man.' Manam is the driving ethic of Sangam warriors — they would accept death before accepting dishonor. Purananuru 312 links it to noble parenthood.
example_sentence: மானம் உடையவர்க்கு மாண்பு குன்றாது.
example_translation: For those who possess self-respect, excellence never diminishes.

english: Moral shame, modesty, shrinking from wrongdoing
definition: The inner restraint that prevents wrong action through moral shame.
historical_meaning: Thirukkural Chapter 102: Nan is one of the four virtues of a nobleman. It is the internal conscience that causes one to shrink from base action even when no one is watching — the foundation of moral integrity.
example_sentence: நாணின் மகிழ்ச்சி நலனில் உழல்வான் வாணிக வாழ்க்கை வழக்கு.
example_translation: The life of one who wanders without modesty is mere commerce without nobility.

definition: Absolute devotion and loyalty to one's spouse as a moral and spiritual power.
historical_meaning: Karpu is the supreme virtue of married women in Classical Tamil. Silappathikaram's Kannaki demonstrates its power: her chastity enables her to burn Madurai. Thirukkural Ch 55: 'She who worships her husband commands the rains.'
example_sentence: தெய்வம் தொழாஅள் கொழுநன் தொழுதெழுவாள் பெய்யெனப் பெய்யும் மழை.
example_translation: The woman who worships her husband rather than the gods commands the rain to fall.

definition: The householder's duty to feed and shelter strangers with generosity.
historical_meaning: Thirukkural Chapter 9: 'All domestic living is for the sake of welcoming guests.' Hospitality was a cardinal virtue — eating alone while a guest waits outside is condemned even if the food is nectar. Purananuru 182 glorifies it.
example_sentence: இருந்தோம்பி இல்வாழ்வ தெல்லாம் விருந்தோம்பி வேளாண்மை செய்தற் பொருட்டு.
example_translation: All domestic living is for the sake of welcoming guests with hospitality.

definition: Refraining from causing pain even to those who have wronged us.
historical_meaning: Thirukkural Chapter 32: 'The cure for those who do evil is not to retaliate but to bring them to shame by doing good.' This principle of non-retaliation is distinct from mere passivity — it is an active moral stance.
example_sentence: அகழ்வாரைத் தாங்கும் நிலம்போல் தம்மை இகழ்வார்ப்பொறுத்தல் தலை.
example_translation: To endure those who insult us is the highest virtue, as the earth bears those who dig it.

english: Noble scholars, the fully virtuous person
definition: The ideal complete human characterized by wisdom, courage, and moral excellence.
historical_meaning: The Sanror (noble one) is the ethical ideal of Sangam society — possessing learning, valor, generosity, and wisdom simultaneously. Purananuru 312: 'To make him a noble man (Sanror) is the father's duty.'
example_sentence: ஈன்று புறந்தருதல் என்தலைக் கடனே சான்றோன் ஆக்குதல் தந்தைக்குக் கடனே.
example_translation: Bearing and nurturing the child is the mother's duty; making him noble (Sanror) is the father's.

definition: Affability, empathy, and cultivated goodness of heart.
historical_meaning: Thirukkural Chapter 100: Panpu is the all-encompassing quality of good character — loving conduct towards all, cultural refinement, and inner goodness. The Sanror is defined partly by their Panpu.
example_sentence: பண்புடையவர் பழகில் பண்பு பெறுவர் மற்றவர்.
example_translation: Those who associate with the noble-charactered will themselves acquire noble character.

definition: Fearlessness in the face of the enemy and death on the battlefield.
historical_meaning: Viram is the defining virtue of Puram poetry. Heroes wore Thumbai flowers and sought glorious death (Maranam) for eternal fame (Pukazh). Purananuru records kings and warriors who achieved immortality through Viram.
example_sentence: விளங்கு தும்பை சூடிப் போர்க்களத்தே உயிர் துறந்தான்.
example_translation: Wearing the bright Thumbai flower, he sacrificed his life on the battlefield.

definition: The symbol of unbiased justice and the king's duty to rule righteously.
historical_meaning: Thirukkural Chapter 55: 'To investigate, be impartial, and administer justice to all is righteous rule.' If the scepter bends (Murankolmai), rains fail and the land suffers. Silappathikaram dramatizes this: Pandyan's unjust execution causes cosmic disorder.
example_sentence: ஓர்ந்துகண் ணோடாது இறைபுரிந்து யார்மாட்டும் தேர்ந்துசெய் வஃதே முறை.
example_translation: Investigation, impartiality, and justice to all is the way of righteous rule.

english: King, ruler, protector of the state
definition: The head of state whose primary duty is the welfare of his people.
historical_meaning: The ideal Sangam king embodies Sengol, Kodai, and Viram. Thirukkural Book 2 dedicates 57 chapters to the king's duties. He is the father of the people, and his virtue determines the prosperity of rain and harvest.
example_sentence: நாடுகாத்து நல்ல வழி நடந்த அரசன் வாழ்க்கை நீடுழி வாழ்க.
example_translation: Long live the king who protects the land and walks the righteous path.

definition: The actions performed by a person which accumulate as karma and determine fate.
historical_meaning: Vinai is both the external work a hero does (Vetchi, war, travel) and the internal karma that shapes destiny. Kurunthokai 135: 'Action is the life of men.' Tolkappiyam classifies Vinai in detail.
example_sentence: வினையே ஆடவர்க்கு உயிரே மனையுறை மகளிர்க்கு ஆடவர் உயிர்.
example_translation: Action is the life of men; for women who remain at home, their husband is their life.

definition: The inner, personal world dealing with love, classified by five landscapes.
historical_meaning: One of the two primary divisions of Classical Tamil literature (with Puram). Tolkappiyam Akattinaiyal classifies Akam into five Tinais, each associated with a landscape, season, flower, bird, and emotional situation — without naming individuals.
related_concepts: Puram, Tinai, Kurinji, Mullai, Neythal, Palai, Marutham
example_sentence: அகப்பொருள் மாந்தரின் உள்ளத்துணர்வுகளை விளக்கும் இலக்கியப் பாகுபாடாகும்.
example_translation: Akam literature is the classification that explains the inner feelings of humans.

definition: The outer, public world of warfare, statecraft, and heroic ethics.
historical_meaning: Tolkappiyam Purattinaiyal classifies Puram into seven warfare stages, each with a symbolic flower worn by the warriors. Kings and heroes are named, unlike Akam poetry. Purananuru, Pathitrupathu, and Agananuru are key texts.
related_concepts: Akam, Tinai, Vetchi, Thumbai, Vakai, Viram
example_sentence: புறப்பொருள் போரையும் வீரத்தையும் கொடையையும் பாடும் மரபாகும்.
example_translation: Puram literature is the tradition of singing about war, valor, and philanthropy.

english: Landscape, genre, category of emotional experience
definition: Tolkappiyam's classification system linking landscape, season, and emotion.
historical_meaning: Tolkappiyam establishes five main Akam Tinais and seven Puram Tinais. Each Akam Tinai maps: place → time → flower → bird → tree → deity → emotional situation. This is the foundational grammar of Sangam poetics.
related_concepts: Akam, Puram, Kurinji, Mullai, Marutham, Neythal, Palai
example_sentence: திணை என்பது இடமும் பொழுதும் உணர்வும் இணைந்த ஓர் இலக்கியக் கோட்பாடு.
example_translation: Tinai is a literary concept where place, time, and emotion are unified.

definition: The hilly landscape associated with Kalavu (secret love) and union.
historical_meaning: Kurinji Tinai: mountains, night/early dawn, Kurinji flower, Kuyil bird. The emotional situation is Kalavu (secret premarital meeting). Kurunthokai 3 and 40 are celebrated examples.
example_sentence: நிலத்தினும் பெரிதே வானினும் உயர்ந்தன்று சாரல் குறிஞ்சிப் பூக்கொண்டு பெருந்தேன் இழைக்கும் நாடனோடு நட்பே.
example_translation: Greater than earth and sky is the friendship with the lord of the Kurinji land.

definition: Forest pastures associated with the heroine's patient wait for the hero's return.
historical_meaning: Mullai Tinai: forest, evening/rainy season, Jasmine flower, Neithal bird. The hero goes away for war or work; the heroine waits with patience (Mella). Ainkurunuru and Natrinai have major Mullai sections.
example_sentence: முல்லை நிலத்து மழை பொழியும் மாலையில் தலைவன் வாரான் என்று தலைவி காத்தாள்.
example_translation: In the Mullai land when evening rains fell, the heroine waited for her love to return.

definition: Fertile river plains associated with infidelity and the wife's righteous jealousy.
historical_meaning: Marutham Tinai: agricultural plains, midday/all seasons, White Marudam flower. The hero strays to a courtesan (Parathai); the wife (Manaivi) shows jealousy (Udal). Kalithokai has extensive Marutham poems.
example_sentence: மருதம் நிலத்தில் மனைவி மனம் கொதித்து ஊடல் கொண்டாள்.
example_translation: In the fertile Marutham land, the wife seethed inwardly and took up a lover's tiff.

definition: The seashore region symbolizing the heroine's grief during separation.
historical_meaning: Neythal Tinai: seashore, evening/all seasons, Blue Lotus flower, Tern bird. The hero delays his return; the heroine weeps by the sea. Narrinai opens with a Neythal poem; Kurunthokai has many.
example_sentence: நெய்தல் நிலத்துத் தலைவி கடலையே நோக்கி நின்றாள்.
example_translation: The heroine of the coastal land stood gazing at the sea for her departed lover.

definition: The desert/wilderness region symbolizing painful separation and dangerous travel.
historical_meaning: Palai Tinai: arid wasteland, noon/hot seasons, Palai tree. The hero departs through the dangerous desert for wealth or war. Akananuru and Narrinai have major Palai sections of grief and longing.
example_sentence: பாலை நிலத்தில் பிரிந்த காதலன் வரும் நாள் வரை பொறுத்திருக்கும் கல்மனம்.
example_translation: In the arid Palai landscape, the heroine's stone-like heart waits for her love to return.

english: Cattle raiding — first stage of war
definition: Invading force wearing Vetchi flowers to steal enemy cattle as the first act of war.
historical_meaning: Tolkappiyam Purattinaiyal establishes seven Puram genres by warfare flower. Vetchi is the first: cattle raiding to provoke conflict. The defenders respond with Karanthai (cattle rescue).
example_sentence: மன்னன் வெட்சி பூவைச் சூடிப் போர்க்களத்திற்குச் சென்றான்.
example_translation: The king went to the battlefield wearing the Vetchi flower.

definition: Defenders wearing Karanthai flowers to rescue raided cattle.
historical_meaning: The second stage of Puram warfare. After Vetchi (cattle raid), the defenders mobilize wearing Karanthai flowers to reclaim their cattle and honor. Part of the ritualized warfare cycle in Sangam culture.
example_sentence: காளையரும் கரந்தை சூடிப் போர்க்களம் புகுந்தனர்.
example_translation: The young warriors wore Karanthai flowers and entered the battlefield.

english: Invasion march — conquest of enemy territory
definition: King marching with troops wearing Vanji flowers to conquer enemy land.
historical_meaning: Vanji is the offensive march of a king to claim dominion. The Chera dynasty capital was named Vanji. Vanji songs in Purananuru celebrate the martial departure of kings for conquest.
example_sentence: வஞ்சிப் பூவைச் சூடிய மன்னன் பகை நாட்டை வென்றான்.
example_translation: The king wearing Vanji flowers conquered the enemy land.

definition: Defending one's territory or reflecting on the transience of all things.
historical_meaning: Kanchi is the meditative warfare genre — the defender's awareness of impermanence, the brevity of victory and life. It is the philosophical layer of Puram poetry, reflecting that all greatness passes. Purananuru has many Kanchi poems.
example_sentence: காஞ்சி நிலையில் வீரன் வாழ்க்கையின் நிலையாமையை உணர்ந்தான்.
example_translation: In the Kanchi stage, the warrior understood the impermanence of life.

definition: Soldiers defending the walls of a fort wearing Nochi leaves.
historical_meaning: Nochi is the Puram genre of fort defense. The defenders within the fort wear Nochi leaves; the besiegers respond with Uzhignai. It represents endurance and the courage of the besieged.
example_sentence: நொச்சி இலையணிந்த வீரர்கள் கோட்டை மதிலில் காவல் நின்றார்.
example_translation: Warriors wearing Nochi leaves stood guard on the fort walls.

definition: Invaders encircling and besieging a fort wearing Uzhignai.
historical_meaning: Uzhignai is the Puram genre of besieging a fort. The invaders wear Uzhignai and attempt to break through. It stands opposite to Nochi (fortress defense).
example_sentence: உழிஞை சூடிய படைகள் கோட்டையை வளைத்தனர்.
example_translation: Armies wearing Uzhignai encircled and besieged the fortress.

definition: Both armies meeting on an open field wearing Thumbai flowers for open warfare.
historical_meaning: Thumbai is the most glorified Puram genre — open pitched battle where both sides agree to fight equally. The warrior who dies in Thumbai wearing its flowers achieves eternal fame (Pukazh) and a hero stone (Naduvaikal).
example_sentence: விளங்கு தும்பை சூடிப் போர்க்களத்தே உயிர் துறந்தான்.
example_translation: Wearing the bright Thumbai flower, he sacrificed his life on the battlefield.

definition: The victorious warriors wearing Vakai flowers to celebrate military triumph.
historical_meaning: Vakai concludes the Puram warfare cycle. Victorious armies wear White Vakai flowers. Vakai songs praise the king's victory in war and the distribution of spoils. It also represents the scholar's victory in learned debate.
example_sentence: வேல்வீரன் வெற்றிக்கு மகிழ்ந்த குடிமக்கள் வாகை மாலை சூடினர்.
example_translation: The people rejoiced at the warrior's victory and wore garlands of Vakai flowers.

definition: Poetic genre praising the fame, power, and gifts of a specific hero.
historical_meaning: Padan is the Puram sub-genre devoted to direct eulogy of a king or warrior. Pathitrupathu is entirely composed of Padan poems for Chera kings. The poet's livelihood depended on the king's Kodai in response to Padan praise.
example_sentence: கபிலர் புலவர் பாண்டியன் நெடுஞ்செழியனை பாடாண் பாட்டில் புகழ்ந்தார்.
example_translation: The poet Kapilar praised King Nedunchezhiyan in a Padan eulogy.

definition: Physical manifestation of laughter — one of eight Meyppadus in Tolkappiyam.
historical_meaning: Tolkappiyam Meyppattiyal classifies eight physical manifestations of emotion (Meyppadu). Nakai is triggered by comic or joyful situations. It corresponds to the Hasya rasa in Sanskrit aesthetics.
example_sentence: கோவலன் நகையுடன் மாதவியின் நடனத்தைப் பார்த்தான்.
example_translation: Kovalan watched Madhavi's dance with laughter and delight.

definition: Physical expression of sorrow — one of eight Meyppadus.
historical_meaning: Alukai is the physical manifestation of weeping triggered by loss, separation, or tragedy. It is the dominant Meyppadu of Neythal and Palai landscapes where the heroine weeps for the absent hero.
example_sentence: தலைவியின் அழுகை கடல் நீரினும் பெரிதாயிற்று.
example_translation: The heroine's weeping became greater than the sea itself.

definition: Physical manifestation of revulsion or degradation — one of eight Meyppadus.
historical_meaning: Ilivaral is triggered by humiliating experiences, degradation, or self-contempt. In Akam poetry it appears when the heroine feels dishonored; in Puram when a warrior faces shame.
example_sentence: வீரன் தன் தோல்வியை கண்டு இழிவரலில் வீழ்ந்தான்.
example_translation: Seeing his defeat, the warrior fell into disgust and shame.

definition: Physical manifestation of awe and surprise — one of eight Meyppadus.
historical_meaning: Marutkai corresponds to the Adbhuta rasa of Sanskrit aesthetics. In Sangam poetry it appears when heroes witness the grandeur of nature, divine acts, or extraordinary valor in battle.
example_sentence: மன்னன் கொடையைக் கண்டு புலவர் மருட்கையில் திகைத்தார்.
example_translation: Seeing the king's generosity, the poet stood in wonder and awe.

definition: Physical manifestation of fear — one of eight Meyppadus.
historical_meaning: Acham corresponds to the Bhayanaka rasa. In Akam poetry, the heroine fears the dangerous landscape (desert, sea) during her lover's absence. In Puram, warriors face Acham before battle but conquer it through Viram.
example_sentence: வீரன் அச்சமின்றி போர்க்களம் புகுந்தான்.
example_translation: The warrior entered the battlefield without fear.

definition: The dignified pride arising from valor, learning, or charitable deeds.
historical_meaning: Perumitham corresponds to Veera rasa. It is the physical manifestation of noble pride — the warrior's upright bearing, the scholar's confident eloquence, the king's dignified generosity. Thirukkural praises this as Manam.
example_sentence: வேந்தன் வெற்றியுடன் பெருமிதத்தோடு வீரர்களை வரவேற்றான்.
example_translation: The king welcomed the warriors with noble pride after the victory.

definition: Physical manifestation of anger — one of eight Meyppadus.
historical_meaning: Vekuli corresponds to Raudra rasa. In Puram poetry it is the righteous anger of the king when injustice is done. In Akam, it is the wife's anger (Udal) in Marutham. Thirukkural warns that uncontrolled Vekuli destroys the self.
example_sentence: நீதி மறுக்கப்பட்டதால் அரசன் வெகுளியில் எழுந்தான்.
example_translation: When justice was denied, the king rose in righteous anger.

definition: Pure joy and satisfaction — one of eight Meyppadus.
historical_meaning: Uvakai corresponds to Shanta rasa or Sringara's joy. It is the physical manifestation of happiness at reunion, gift-receiving, or victory. Koodal (union) in Kurinji Tinai is the primary context for Uvakai in Akam poetry.
example_sentence: தலைவன் திரும்பி வந்ததால் தலைவி உவகையில் மகிழ்ந்தாள்.
example_translation: The heroine rejoiced with delight when her love returned.

definition: The righteous path of married household life.
historical_meaning: Thirukkural Chapter 5 celebrates Illaram as superior to asceticism if lived virtuously. 'The householder is the support of the other three orders of life.' It balances Aram (virtue), love (Anbu), and social duty (Ozhukkam).
example_sentence: இல்வாழ்வான் என்பான் இயல்புடைய மூவர்க்கும் நல்லாற்றின் நின்ற துணை.
example_translation: The householder is the pillar of support for the other three righteous life-paths.

definition: The path of detachment from worldly bonds pursued for spiritual liberation.
historical_meaning: Thirukkural Chapter 35: Turavaram is praised as the highest path, but Illaram lived virtuously is its equal. Manimekalai's heroine renounces domestic life to pursue Turavaram for universal compassion.
example_sentence: மணிமேகலை இல்வாழ்க்கையை துறந்து துறவறம் மேற்கொண்டாள்.
example_translation: Manimekalai renounced domestic life and embraced the ascetic path.

english: Female confidante, companion of the heroine
definition: The heroine's close friend who advises, protects, and acts as her messenger.
historical_meaning: The Thozhi is an indispensable character in Akam poetry. She mediates between the lovers, consoles the heroine during separation, and often speaks more poems than the heroine herself. Tolkappiyam defines her role precisely.
source_reference: Tolkappiyam Akattinaiyal; all Five Tinai collections
example_sentence: தோழி இன்சொல் பேசி தலைவியின் துயரை தணித்தாள்.
example_translation: The confidante spoke sweetly and alleviated the heroine's grief.

definition: A singer and messenger who carries the hero's message to the heroine.
historical_meaning: The Panan played the Yazh and sang for kings and lovers alike. In Puram, he received Kodai (gifts) for his poems. In Akam, he carried the hero's messages through the dangerous Palai landscape.
example_sentence: பாணன் யாழிசையால் மன்னனின் புகழை பாடி கொடை பெற்றான்.
example_translation: The minstrel sang the king's praises with the Yazh and received generous gifts.

definition: A female performer who danced and sang, accompanying the male Panan.
historical_meaning: The Virali was the female counterpart of the Panan. She danced while the Panan sang. Together they traveled between courts to receive royal patronage. Many Purananuru poems address or praise the Virali's skills.
example_sentence: விறலி நடனம் ஆடி மன்னனின் மனத்தை மகிழ்வித்தாள்.
example_translation: The female dancer danced and delighted the king's heart.

definition: A chieftain or king famous for extraordinary generosity to poets and the poor.
historical_meaning: The title Vallal was given to kings and chieftains who gave unstintingly. The Seven Great Tamil Patrons (Kaadai Ezhu Vallalgal) are the most celebrated: Pari, Kari, Ori, Pegan, Ay, Athigan, Nalli.
example_sentence: வள்ளலின் கொடை கேட்டு புலவர்கள் தொலைவிலிருந்து வந்தனர்.
example_translation: Hearing the philanthropist's generosity, poets came from afar.

definition: The concept of all humanity as one's kinsmen.
historical_meaning: Purananuru 192 by Kaniyan Pungundranar: 'Yaathum oore, yaavarum kelir' — 'Every city is mine, all people are my kinsmen.' This is the most radical statement of universal brotherhood in ancient Tamil thought.
example_sentence: யாதும் ஊரே யாவரும் கேளிர்.
example_translation: Every city is my native place; all people are my kinsmen.

definition: A learned poet composing in honor of truth, beauty, and human experience.
historical_meaning: The Sangam Pulavan was not just a poet but a moral philosopher and historian. Poets like Kapilar, Paranar, and Avvaiyar shaped the ethical discourse of their age. They depended on royal patronage (Kodai) for their livelihood.
example_sentence: புலவன் தன் பாடலில் மன்னனின் வீரத்தையும் கொடையையும் போற்றினான்.
example_translation: The poet praised the king's valor and generosity in his poem.

definition: The primary literary device comparing emotions to nature in Akam poetry.
historical_meaning: Tolkappiyam Porulatikaram specifies hundreds of permitted Uvamam (similes) for each Tinai. Each landscape has its own sanctioned set of comparisons. Kurunthokai 40's red earth simile is the most celebrated example.
example_sentence: செம்புலப் பெயல்நீர் போல அன்புடை நெஞ்சம் தாங்கலந்த னவே.
example_translation: Like red soil mixing with pouring rain, our loving hearts have merged into one.

definition: An embedded metaphor in Akam poems where nature parallels human emotion.
historical_meaning: Ullurai is a sophisticated Tolkappiyam device where the natural image and the human emotional situation are not directly compared (as in Uvamam) but run as parallel narratives that illuminate each other implicitly.
example_sentence: உள்ளுறை என்பது இயற்கையையும் மனிதனையும் ஒரு நேரத்தில் கூறும் நுட்பமான உத்தி.
example_translation: Ullurai is the subtle device that simultaneously describes nature and the human heart.

definition: The primary stringed instrument of Classical Tamil culture.
historical_meaning: The Yazh was the principal instrument of Sangam music. The Panan played it while accompanying the king or carrying messages for lovers. Silappathikaram extensively describes various types of Yazh and musical scales (Pan).
example_sentence: பாணன் யாழிசையால் தலைவியின் காதலனை அழைத்தான்.
example_translation: The minstrel summoned the heroine's lover with the music of the Yazh.

definition: Musical scales corresponding to landscapes and emotional states.
historical_meaning: Each Tinai had its own sanctioned Pan (melody). Silappathikaram describes seven Pans for the seven Tinais. The most celebrated is Kurinji-Pan for mountain love songs. Tolkappiyam's Marabu (tradition) encoded this system.
example_sentence: குறிஞ்சி பண்ணில் பாடப்பட்ட பாட்டு காதலரின் நெஞ்சை நெகிழ்த்தது.
example_translation: The song sung in the Kurinji melody melted the lovers' hearts.

definition: Theatrical dance performance depicting epics and stories.
historical_meaning: Koothu was the primary dramatic tradition of the Sangam era. Silappathikaram's Madhavi is a master of Koothu — the text lists numerous types of dance including Arangetram (debut performance).
example_sentence: மாதவி அரங்கேற்றத்தில் கூத்து நடனம் ஆடி மன்னனை மகிழ்வித்தாள்.
example_translation: Madhavi performed the Koothu dance at her debut and delighted the king.

definition: A structured canonical text containing moral codes, grammar, or science.
historical_meaning: Nool is the term for authoritative texts: Tolkappiyam (grammar/poetics), Thirukkural (ethics), Silappathikaram (epic). Thirukkural opens with praise of God as 'the beginning' — a Nool of Nools.
example_sentence: நூலறிந்து நன்மை செய்வோரே உலகில் வாழும் பயனுடையோர்.
example_translation: Those who know the canonical texts and do good are the ones who live worthily.

definition: The strict metrical form of Thirukkural, characterized by rhyming couplets.
historical_meaning: Venpa is one of the four main Tamil meters (Pa). Thirukkural uses Kuttraalenba (a type of Venpa) with strict rules: 4 feet per line, 2 lines per couplet, a unique 'kural' foot in the second line. It is the most demanding and prestigious Sangam meter.
example_sentence: வெண்பா அமைப்பில் திருவள்ளுவர் மனித நேயத்தை பாடினார்.
example_translation: In the Venpa meter, Thiruvalluvar sang of human virtue.

definition: The male partner in marriage and head of the household.
historical_meaning: In Sangam poetry, the Kanavan is the hero who leaves for war or wealth (Palai, Mullai). Kurunthokai 135: 'Action is the life of men; for women at home, their Kanavan is life.' Thirukkural Ch 6 describes the ideal mutual relationship.
example_sentence: கணவன் வீடு திரும்பும் நாளை தலைவி ஆவலுடன் எதிர்பார்த்தாள்.
example_translation: The heroine eagerly awaited the day her husband would return home.

definition: The female partner in marriage and guardian of domestic virtue.
historical_meaning: The Manaivi is the central figure of Thirukkural Chapter 6 (Vaazhkkai Thunainalm). Her virtue (Karpu) and management of the household (Mana-maadchi) determine the family's prosperity and the husband's honor.
example_sentence: மனைமாட்சி இல்லாள்கண் இல்லாயின் வாழ்க்கை எனைமாட்சித்து ஆயினும் இல்.
example_translation: If the wife lacks home-adorning virtue, however grand the life, it has no real worth.

definition: Mutual romantic and passionate affection between a man and a woman.
historical_meaning: Kadal (love) is the soul of all Akam poetry. It encompasses the full range from secret meeting (Kalavu/Kurinji) to marital union (Karpu) and longing during separation. Kurunthokai 3 is the supreme expression.
source_reference: Tolkappiyam; Kurunthokai 3, 40; all Five Tinai collections
example_sentence: நிலத்தினும் பெரிதே வானினும் உயர்ந்தன்று குறிஞ்சி நாடனோடு நட்பே.
example_translation: Greater than earth and higher than sky is my love for the lord of the Kurinji land.

definition: The hidden, clandestine phase of love before parental approval and marriage.
historical_meaning: Tolkappiyam defines Kalavu as the first phase of Akam love — secret meetings in the mountainous Kurinji landscape at night. It precedes Karpu (married fidelity). The Thozhi mediates between the lovers during Kalavu.
example_sentence: களவு ஒழுக்கத்தில் தலைவன் தலைவியை இரவில் மலைச்சரிவில் சந்தித்தான்.
example_translation: In the convention of secret love, the hero met the heroine at night on the hillside.

definition: The happy coming-together of separated lovers.
historical_meaning: Koodal is the opposite of Pirivu (separation). It is the emotional climax of the lovers' journey — whether the first union in Kurinji or the return from Palai separation. Uvakai (delight) is its defining Meyppadu.
example_sentence: நெடுநாள் பிரிவுக்கு பிறகு தலைவன் வந்ததால் கூடல் இனிதாயிற்று.
example_translation: After a long separation, the hero's return made the union sweet.

definition: The hero's departure for war, education, or wealth — the source of the heroine's grief.
historical_meaning: Pirivu is the emotional core of Palai, Neythal, and Mullai Tinais. The hero's separation generates the heroine's longing and the Thozhi's consolation. Narrinai, Kurunthogai, and Akananuru are full of Pirivu imagery.
example_sentence: பிரிவின் துயரில் தலைவி கண்ணீர் கடலினும் பெரிதாயிற்று.
example_translation: In the sorrow of separation, the heroine's tears became greater than the sea.

english: Lover's tiff, feigned anger in marriage
definition: Temporary romantic disagreement or feigned anger between married lovers.
historical_meaning: Thirukkural Chapter 131 praises Udal as the 'pepper' that spices married life. In Marutham Tinai, the wife's Udal is her righteous response to infidelity. It is a sophisticated convention of Akam poetry, not mere quarreling.
example_sentence: ஊடல் உவகை தரும் இல்லறத்தின் இனிய நிகழ்வாகும்.
example_translation: The lover's tiff is a sweet event of domestic life that eventually brings joy.

definition: Memorial stone erected to commemorate heroes who died in battle.
historical_meaning: Naduvaikal is a uniquely Tamil institution documented in Purananuru and in archaeological finds across Tamil Nadu. After a hero's death in Thumbai combat, a stone was inscribed, worshipped, and sung to. It is the physical form of Pukazh.
example_sentence: போர்க்களத்தில் மாண்ட வீரனுக்கு நடுகல் நட்டு வணங்கினர்.
example_translation: They erected and worshipped a hero stone for the warrior who fell on the battlefield.

english: Assembly, royal court, council of elders
definition: The public assembly of learned men, poets, and nobles where discourse happens.
historical_meaning: Thirukkural Chapter 73 covers Avai: how to speak in assemblies. The Sangam literary academies (Sangams) were the supreme Avais. The Avai was the crucible of Tamil intellectual life.
example_sentence: பல்லார் முனியப் பயனில சொல்லுவான் எல்லாரும் எள்ளப் படும்.
example_translation: He who speaks useless words to the disgust of the assembly will be despised by all.

definition: The mythical or historical academies of Tamil poets that produced the classical corpus.
historical_meaning: The Three Sangams (Muthal, Idai, Kadai Sangam) of Madurai are described in later Tamil tradition as academies under royal patronage that preserved the classical corpus. The term gives the Sangam era its name.
example_sentence: மதுரையில் நடைபெற்ற சங்கத்தில் தமிழ்ப் புலவர்கள் கூடிப் பாடினர்.
example_translation: Tamil poets gathered at the Sangam in Madurai and composed their poems.

definition: A prosperous territory governed by a just king with fertile land and happy people.
historical_meaning: Thirukkural Chapter 74 defines the ideal Nadu: fortress, food, wealth, army, allies, and wise counsel. The king's virtue determines the Nadu's prosperity — even rainfall depends on the ruler's righteousness.
example_sentence: வளம் கெழு நாட்டில் மக்கள் மகிழ்வாக வாழ்ந்தனர்.
example_translation: People lived happily in the prosperous and fertile land.

definition: A natural or constructed fortification protecting the kingdom.
historical_meaning: Thirukkural Chapter 75 defines the ideal Aran. Three types exist: natural (mountains, rivers), constructed (walls), and divine. The greatest Aran is the king's virtue — 'Aram is the fortress of virtuous life.'
example_sentence: அரணின் வலிமை படை வலிமையிலும் அரசனின் நேர்மையிலும் அமைந்திருக்கிறது.
example_translation: The strength of a fortress lies in the strength of the army and the king's integrity.

definition: The absolute truth or physical reality of existence.
historical_meaning: Mey has two primary meanings: (1) the body — the physical vessel, and (2) truth — the absolute reality beyond illusion. In Tolkappiyam Meyppattiyal, 'Meyppadu' (physical manifestation) derives from Mey.
example_sentence: மெய்யறிந்தோர் வாய்ச்சொல் செம்பொருள் ஆகும்.
example_translation: The words of those who have known truth become the true meaning itself.

definition: The animating principle that distinguishes living beings from the non-living.
historical_meaning: Tolkappiyam distinguishes Uyir (soul/life) from Mey (body). Thirukkural constantly references Uyir as the precious gift to be protected. Sangam poetry frames all ethical values in relation to the Uyir's welfare.
example_sentence: உயிர்க்கு அறம் செய்தல் உயர்ந்த கடன் ஆகும்.
example_translation: Performing virtue for all living souls is the highest duty.

definition: The primary weapon of the Tamil warrior and divine symbol of Lord Murugan.
historical_meaning: The Vel is both a weapon and a symbol. As the weapon of Murugan (the Tamil deity), it represents divine justice. Warriors carried Vel as a sacred symbol. Sangam poems describe its golden shaft and its terrifying power in battle.
example_sentence: வேல் ஏந்தி வீரன் போர்க்களம் புகுந்தான்.
example_translation: Holding the spear, the warrior entered the battlefield.

definition: Ritual fire sacrifices performed by kings and priests to ensure cosmic order.
historical_meaning: Velvi (Yajna) was performed by Sangam kings to secure divine favor, rain, and victory. Purananuru 15: Kapilar praises the king who performed many Velvis. Five types of Velvi are also listed in Thirukkural Ch 5.
example_sentence: நன்பல வேள்வி செய்து முடித்த அரசனைக் கபிலர் பாராட்டினார்.
example_translation: Kapilar praised the king who successfully performed many noble fire sacrifices.
